REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (144 O'Shaughnessy Blvd, Sf Ca 94107)
Off Calendar May-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for (administrator) Debra Dolch. Stephanie Hess (via court call) for (petitioner) Evelyn Velasquez.

The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $1,100,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 5/5/16. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount to be determined is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo for and with (petitioner) Martha Francis. Stephanie Hess (via court call) and Mia Shirley for (petitioner) Evelyn Velasquez.

The Court asked if the parties were in agreement regarding the special administrator. Ms. Mayo stated that they are not, that they had a short mediation with Ms. Pantazis, that it had to be truncated and they were given a date to return, that opposing counsel was unable to attend on that date, that the house is insured and vacant, that they are ready to proceed and that there has been no malfeasance and no one has accessed the accounts. Ms. Shirley stated that mediation was too early, that Ms. Velasquez is still objecting to the appointment of Ms. Francis, that a neutral administrator seems like the best option, that there has been a long delay since the death of the decedent and that they need to get this administration started to get a picture of what is in the estate. Ms. Mayo stated that as soon as Ms. Francis has letters they will file the appropriate affidavits, will list the property and expect to have this estate completed within 9 months. Ms. Hess stated that they need a neutral administrator to investigate the claims that appear to exist, that assets have been used by Ms. Francis for the last 25 years, that they have recently communicated with opposing counsel but are not getting cooperation, that the do not know the state of the property, that it looks like the insurance was just obtained in December and that hostilities exist between the family members. Ms. Mayo stated that they have provided extensive photos of the property, that a site inspection will be performed this month, that everyone was given the opportunity to live in the house and that insurance was obtained once the house was vacant and is for the dwelling only. The Court asked about the parties process for identifying a neutral administrator. Ms. Mayo stated that if the parties do not agree the court could appoint one. Ms. Shirley that that they would like Caron Schmierer appointed and that she is available and that they would like someone familiar with this court system. Ms. Mayo stated that there is no need for a Special Administrator and that they request that the parties co-administer the estate. Ms. Shirley stated that this would not be acceptable, that there is quite a bit of animosity and that the family members to not talk to each other. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary and the petition for letters of administration were continued to January 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to notify the court of their choice of a neutral administrator or for the court to appoint one. The parties are also instructed to continue to mediate and to submit a report on the status of mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Jan-04-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo, for Martha Sanchez; Stephanie Hess (by CourtCall) for Evelyn Velasquez

Ms. Mayo stated thet the parties were stipll working on resolving the matter; and she was hopeful of having some agreement in place within two weeks. The Court stated his concern that with the intervening holidays, planning on having a resolution within two weeks might be unduly ambitious. Ms, Hess agreed that the parties wanted to get the property on the market soon, but conceeded that the two-week period for resolution was ambitions. Following additional discussion regarding the length of a continuance, upon agreement of the parties the matters were continued to January 4, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported).

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Jan-04-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo, for Petitioner Martha Francis (by CourtCall); Stephanie Hess, for Petitioner Evelyn Velazquez (by CourtCall).

Ms. Hess stated that the parties were working together trying to settle the issues, and requested that the hearing be continued for a period of one week. The Court expressed concern that one week might not be sufficient. Ms. Mayo confirmed that she also preferred a brief continuance, as it would be beneficial to get the property on the market as soon as possible. The matter was continued to December 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
